title: Business Outlook Survey
summary: The Bank of Canada Business Outlook Survey for Q4 2025 shows business sentiment remains subdued, with firms expecting only slight improvement in domestic sales after a weak year and modest export growth. Most businesses plan to maintain or reduce staffing levels while prioritizing routine maintenance investments, suggesting caution about expansion.
key_points: Business sentiment remains subdued according to Q4 2025 Business Outlook Survey and Business Leaders' Pulse
Domestic sales growth expected to improve slightly after a weak year; export sales expected to remain modest
Most businesses plan to maintain or decrease current staffing levels
Investment spending continues to prioritize routine maintenance over expansion
Tariff-related cost increase pressures have decreased compared to the prior quarter
Inflation expectations remain stable in the 2.5% to 3% range
Most firms do not anticipate substantial increases in selling prices
why_it_matters: This survey provides forward-looking indicators on Canadian economic activity, employment intentions, and price pressures. Subdued sentiment combined with plans to reduce staffing and limit investment suggests weak domestic demand ahead. Stable but elevated inflation expectations (2.5-3%) give the Bank of Canada room to monitor for demand weakness. Bond and CAD traders will watch for confirmation that the soft patch is temporary versus structural.
macro_context: Canadian business sentiment remains weak with muted hiring and investment intentions. The slight expected improvement in domestic sales after a weak year indicates the economy is not deteriorating further but lacks momentum for strong growth. Inflation expectations at 2.5-3% remain above the Bank of Canada's 2% target, suggesting the BoC will remain cautious about easing further. Reduced tariff-related cost pressures and limited pricing power suggest inflation will remain contained, supporting a patient monetary policy approach.
